title: Measure chlorophyll-a and pheophytin-a by Turner Designs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ddf","text":"Here we describe a protocol for measuring chlorophyll-a and pheophytin-a from microalgae by using Turner Designs (10-AU)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7kc5c","text":"The entire procedure should be carried out as much as possible in subdued light (Green) to prevent photodecomposition.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8mdbk","text":"All glassware should be clean and acid free to prevent  chlorophyll-a degradation.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"61e8u","text":"Waste disposal:","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d3d6d","text":"        Follow all laboratory waste disposal guidelines regarding the disposal of acetone, DMSO solutions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Primary stock: ≅10 mg/L
Saturated magnesium carbonate solution
Extract samples on the filter
Allow Turner to warm-up for at least 15 minutes.
90% buffered acetone
Acetone/DMSO extraction solvent
0.1 N HCl

Use the extraction of the blank filter to zero the instrument at the sensitivity setting intended for sample analysis. Alternatively, record the reading from the blank filter extraction and subtract it from the reading of the sample extraction.
Chlorophyll-a [ug/L]=r*(R_b-R_a)*DF/(r-1)
Add 10 grams magnesium carbonate to 1000 mL of MilliQ water. Magnesium carbonate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M7179-500G (CAS: 23389-33-5)
Settle the solution for a minimum of 24 hours.
100 mL clear “powder free” magnesium carbonate solution
900 mL HPLC grade acetone Acetone Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #270725-4X2L (CAS: 67-64-1)
Mix in an amber reagent bottle.

Mix three parts 90% acetone and two parts of HPLC grade DMSO (Shoaf and Lium 1975) DMSO (CAS grade) Contributed by users (CAS: 67-68-5) Shoaf WT, Lium BW. Improved extraction of chl and b from algae using dimethyl sulfoxide. Limnology and Oceanography. https://doi.org/10.4319/lo.1976.21.6.0926
Dissolve one part of 12 N HCl in 119 parts of MilliQ water 12 N Hydrochloric acid Contributed by users (CAS: 7647-01-0)
Dissolve 1 mg Chlorophyll-a in a 100 mL glass volumetric flask by 90% acetone, top to 100 mL. Chlorophyll-a from spinach Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C5753-1MG (CAS: 479-61-8)
Aliquote 5 mL to Amber vial with glass serological pipet (5 mL)
Store at -80 °C to -50 °C Flashpoint of acetone is -20 °C
Second calibration standard: (≅400 ug/L)

Warm up primary stock to room temperature and transfer 4 mL primary stock to a 100 mL glass volumetric flask Adjust the volume of primary stock according to the actual concentration of second calibration standard
Top with 90% acetone to 100 mL
Working calibration standard (≅16 ug/L) Maximum concentration of detection range is 15~20 ug/L

Fill cuvette and place it in udrop plate, measure absorbance at 664 nm Spectrophotometer Cells (12.5W x 12.5L x 48H mm (pathlength 10 mm), VWR® Spectrosil, 414004-078) µDrop™ Plates (Thermo Scientific, N12391) Varioskan LUX Multimode Microplate Reader (Thermo Fisher, VL0L00D0)
Chlorophyll-a_mg/L = 11.42 X Absorbance at 664 nm, where 11.42 is the extinction coefficient of chlorophyll-a at 664 nm. Detection limit of spectrophotometrical measurement is about 350 ug/L
Label Amber vials with the actual concentration of chlorophyll-a stock solution Storage Vials and Closures (12 mL amber, Thermo Scientific, B7800-12A, VWR 66030-686)
Aliquote 5 mL to amber vials, label cryobox with actual concentration and store at -80 °C to -50 °C .

Warm up second calibration standard and transfer 2 mL solution to a 50 mL glass volumetric flask Adjust the volume of second calibration standard according to the actual concentration of second calibration standard
Top with 90% acetone to 50 mL

For samples collected on GF/F filters, transfer them into 10 mL centrifuge tubes. For samples collected on polycarbonate filters, transfer them into 12 mL amber vials. Storage Vials and Closures (12 mL amber, Thermo Scientific, B7800-12A, VWR 66030-686)
Dispense solvent into each vial or tube. Vortex.
Allow the samples to be extracted for 00:20:00 at Room temperature , ensuring the process is conducted in the dark.
Vortex samples and keep samples in the dark. Allow samples to be extracted for another 00:10:00
Extract liquid samples
Measure absorbance at 664 nm.
Chlorophyll-a_mg/L = 11.42 X Absorbance at 664 nm, where 11.42 is the extinction coefficient of chlorophyll-a at 664 nm. Detection limit of spectrophotometrical measurement is about 350 ug/L
Freeze liquid sample and blank
Add extraction solvent into frozen sample (V_sample) : (V_solvent)= 1:25 ~ 1:50
Cap the vial, vortex and place sample in the dark at room temperature for 00:20:00
Vortex and place sample in the dark for another 00:10:00
Standards Room temperature
Place the solid secondary standard in Turner, wait 15 seconds for the reading to stabilize.
Record the reading on the solid standard calibration record sheet.
Reading should be less than 10% of the previously determined post calibration value. Otherwise, calibrate Turner by liquid standard before measuring samples (Go to the last step: Calibrate Turner)
Primary Chlorophyll a standards Chlorophyll-a standard Contributed by users Catalog # E-541-0-850

Come as a set of one low (15 ~ 20 ug/L) and one high (140 ~ 160 ug/L) concentration in foil wrapped ampules. Use low concentration for the calibration only. Actual concentration varies by LOT, and is listed on a certificate
Break the ampule with breaker and pour directly into the test tube
Blank: 90% buffered acetone Room temperature
Press <ENT> to reach Main Menu screen.
Press <2> to reach the Calibration Menu screen. Set the concentration range control to AUTO.
From screen 2.0, press <4> to bring up screen 2.4, then <3> to bring up screen 2.43 (set conc. range control), and press <ENT> to toggle.

Press <Esc> to screen 2.0, press <2> to access screen 2.2 (standard solution concentration). Enter the actual concentration of the primary liquid calibration standard (e.g. the value from the certificate).
Press <Esc> to return to screen 2.0.
Press <1> to access screen 2.1 and confirm that option #2 on screen 2.1 reads YES.
Press <Esc> to return to HOME screen.

While on the HOME screen, fill a clean 13-mm culture tube with 90% acetone (the blank). Put the tube in the sample chamber and replace the light cap. Because temperature affects fluorescence, do not allow the blank to remain in the instrument any longer than necessary for a stable reading.
Press <ENT> , <2>, <1>, and <1> to access 2.11 After the Blank % reading is stable (“TC” on screen 2.11 cycles from 1 to 8 seconds) and assuming the Blank % is less than 200%, press <0>.
When “FINISHED” appears, press <ESC> all the way to HOME.
Remove the blank. Set tube aside.

While on the HOME screen, place the tube with the LOW primary chlorophyll a standard in the sample chamber and replace the light cap. Because temperature affects fluorescence, do not allow the blank to remain in the instrument any longer than necessary for a stable reading.
Pressing <ENT>, <2>, and <3> to access 2.3
Using UP and DOWN arrows to adjust Span% until the FS reading is the closest to actual value of primary standard.
Wait until reading is stable (“TC” on screen cycles from 1 to 8 sec.), then press <*>.
When “FINISHED” appears, press <ESC> all the way to HOME screen.
Read the primary liquid standard on the home screen by pressing <*>, to confirm that the calibration was properly set and record the calibrant and reading on the calibration log sheet.

Place the solid secondary standard in the instrument, read the LOW value. Record the reading on the solid standard calibration record sheet as the reference of daily check. 10AU Solid secondary standards (Turner Designs Digital Fluorometer, Turner designs, 10-AU-904)

Use polypropylene pipet tip to transfer 5 mL 90% acetone to disposable glass tube, wipe the outside of the tube dry with kimwipe and place in the instrument. Replace the light cap. Culture Tubes, (Disposable, Borosilicate Glass, VWR, 47729-572)
Wait about 15 seconds for the reading to stabilize, and zero the instrument on the sensitivity setting.
Transfer 5 mL working calibration standard (prepared in go to step #7 ) to a disposable glass tube, measure the fluorescence. Log the reading as "standard before acidification (R b )".
Add 150 ul 0.1N HCl to working calibration standard.
Carefully mix solution by vortexing for 10 seconds and measure the fluorescence again. Log the reading as "standard after acidification (R a )".
Calculate the ratio, r, as follows: r=Rb/Ra=7.66/3.70=2.07
Transfer the extract from samples collected on PC filters or the supernatant from samples collected on GF/F filters into a disposable glass tube. Cap the tube and vortex gently.

If the display reads OVER, dilute the sample and reread. Log dilution factor (DF) and the reading (R b ). Always adjust the total volume of the diluted solution to 5 mL to avoid the need for adjusting the volume of 0.1 N HCl.
Add 150 ul 0.1N HCl. Carefully mix solution by vortexing for 10 seconds and keep it in the dark.
pheophytin-a [ug/L]=r*(rR_a-R_b)*DF/(r-1)

Adjust the dispensette to dispense 5 mL of solvent. Purge the dispensette to ensure accurate measurement, then dispense the solvent into a dry 10 mL glass graduated cylinder. Record the actual volume dispensed.
Continue measuring R b from the remaining samples, and store the acidified extracts in the dark.
Measure the fluorescence of the acidified extracts and record the reading (R a ).
For samples collected on GF/F filters, centrifuge 3200 rpm, °C, 5 minutes
creator: Yingyu Hu
